jokes.text: Can't beat a horse. A horse is a bike that pedals itself.
jokes.setup: Discussion about cycling and bikes
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical logic comparing horses to self-pedaling bikes
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist observation with good economy but lacks character specificity

jokes.text: His ass was on that seat? All right!
jokes.setup: Discussing Lance Armstrong's bike model
jokes.punchline: Kevin's inappropriate excitement about Armstrong's actual contact with the seat
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor that feels naturally Kevin-esque

jokes.text: I'm on Sheryl Crow's side in that whole thing, so I feel really weird right now.
jokes.setup: Lance Armstrong bike discussion
jokes.punchline: Reference to Armstrong-Crow relationship making the bike situation awkward
jokes.explanation: Smart reference humor with good economy and unexpected connection

jokes.text: I had those extra wheels on the back that support you.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he knows how to ride a bike
jokes.punchline: Revealing he only rode with training wheels
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - overconfident claim immediately undercut by childish admission

jokes.text: Michael crashes into car while learning to ride bike
jokes.setup: Michael attempting to ride a bike with everyone cheering
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of Michael crashing into a car
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy payoff to the bike setup, predictable but effective

jokes.text: You never forget.
jokes.setup: Michael just crashed while riding a bike
jokes.punchline: Deadpan delivery of the classic saying after clearly forgetting how to ride
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic understatement with maximum economy - three words that completely undercut the preceding action

jokes.text: How do I look? Amazing. How do I look? Normal. Ugly.
jokes.setup: Andy asking for appearance validation
jokes.punchline: Oscar's blunt, escalating honesty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure with Oscar's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: It's weird if I come in slightly after.
jokes.setup: Andy waiting to enter with someone else
jokes.punchline: Overthinking the social dynamics of entrance timing
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment showing his social anxiety and overthinking

jokes.text: He's not 'the' Steve Nash. He's... He's big, though. He's kind of like Scranton's Steve Nash.
jokes.setup: Discussing client Steve Nash
jokes.punchline: Jim's attempt to make a basketball reference locally relevant
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about making inappropriate comparisons

jokes.text: No. No? Nothing? No, Mr. Jock Hipster.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to explain who Steve Nash is
jokes.punchline: Andy's dismissive 'Mr. Jock Hipster' label
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult that reveals Andy's pretensions

jokes.text: Big, red mazel tov to the Libster.
jokes.setup: Reading about someone's wedding
jokes.punchline: Andy's awkward attempt at Jewish congratulations plus nickname
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy pretentiousness - using Jewish phrase incorrectly plus made-up nickname

jokes.text: It appears Dan's Sherpa survived to tell the tale... Oh, my God! What? Is Dan okay? No, he died.
jokes.setup: Andy reading about climbing accident with dramatic flair
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that Dan actually died after Jim's concern
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor misdirection - builds concern then casually reveals death

jokes.text: Broccoli Rob extended quote about milk awareness song
jokes.setup: Andy reading about his college rival's success
jokes.punchline: The absurdly pretentious nature of a 'milk awareness song' collaboration
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about pretentious artistic collaboration, but quote runs long

jokes.text: You know, I forget about milk. This is a terrific reminder.
jokes.setup: Andy's enthusiasm about Broccoli Rob's milk song
jokes.punchline: Jim's perfectly deadpan fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim deadpan response - completely sincere delivery of absurd statement

jokes.text: I was the artsy musical one. In Here Comes Treble, I had four solos, Broccoli Rob had three. Right?
jokes.setup: Andy talking about his college rival
jokes.punchline: His petty counting of solos and seeking validation
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Andy's competitive insecurity and need for validation

jokes.text: Also, he slept with Pam. No, he didn't.
jokes.setup: Dwight listing Danny's negative qualities
jokes.punchline: Jim's immediate correction of Dwight's assumption
jokes.explanation: Good quick correction showing Jim's protective instincts and Dwight's assumptions

jokes.text: That's fizzling. I mean, someone has to start the fizzle.
jokes.setup: Jim thinking Pam initiated the end of her relationship with Danny
jokes.punchline: Pam's logic about how 'fizzling' actually works
jokes.explanation: Smart wordplay that takes a common expression and applies actual logic to it

jokes.text: You know I have a kid with you, right? Aah!
jokes.setup: Jim getting jealous about Pam's past with Danny
jokes.punchline: Pam's reality check and Jim's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Good relationship comedy - Pam grounding Jim's irrational jealousy with reality

jokes.text: So anyway, she says, 'That is the biggest penis I have ever seen.' And I said, 'I know. That's why I brought you to the penis museum, where tickets are $1,000.'
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to intimidate Danny with a 'cool' story
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd story about an expensive penis museum
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Dwight moment - completely inappropriate story with ridiculous details that only he would tell

jokes.text: You are busy! We're in a meeting! What? It's Jim, okay? Yes.
jokes.setup: Jim calling during Dwight's meeting
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confusion about who he's talking to while on the phone
jokes.explanation: Standard phone confusion bit, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Yes. What'd he say? What did he say? The big gun thing. Stop.
jokes.setup: Jim asking Dwight to call Michael 'the big guns'
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal repetition and Jim's embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Basic character comedy showing Dwight's literalness, but predictable

jokes.text: You don't look reluctant, Michael. You look really eager. No, I don't have time for this. Are you kidding me?
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he was reluctantly called back to sales
jokes.punchline: Pam calling out his obvious eagerness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Pam puncturing Michael's false modesty

jokes.text: Not to go all Sherlock Holmes on you, but I can tell by the reflection in your glasses that you're entering points into weightwatchers.com. If you don't enter them immediately, you forget.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming to be a detective
jokes.punchline: Revealing Kevin's Weight Watchers habit through reflection reading
jokes.explanation: Clever observational humor with good character integration and unexpected detective work

jokes.text: How much? 60 bucks a session. That's crazy money. I'll take 40.
jokes.setup: Andy offering Kevin money to join his band
jokes.punchline: Kevin negotiating DOWN from Andy's offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - misunderstanding how negotiations work in his favor

jokes.text: I could swear that guy was a male model.
jokes.setup: Michael seeing Danny for the first time
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate assumption based on looks
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag about Danny's good looks, functional but predictable

jokes.text: It's like if Michael Phelps came out of retirement, jumped in the pool, belly-flopped and drowned.
jokes.setup: Jim talking about how he feels about losing the sale
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dark and escalating sports metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating metaphor that goes darker than expected - from comeback to death

jokes.text: Why don't you go outside and take a shot of insulin and have a nap, okay? Why do you always assume I have diabetes?
jokes.setup: Michael frustrated with Stanley's lack of enthusiasm
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate diabetes assumption and Stanley calling it out
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Michael's inappropriate assumptions and stereotyping

jokes.text: I could try to seduce him. Oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Michael asking how to combat Danny
jokes.punchline: Phyllis's unexpected suggestion and Michael's horror
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven cringe with Phyllis's inappropriate confidence

jokes.text: Like the movie. I think you mean The Sting. Paul Newman, Robert Redford. They're bank robbers? Nope. Different movie. It's called The Stinger. The Sting.
jokes.setup: Michael referencing a classic con movie
jokes.punchline: Michael getting the title wrong and insisting on the wrong name
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael moment - confidently wrong about pop culture references

jokes.text: Meredith Van Helsing? Van Helsing was a respected professor before he was a vampire killer. Okay. But what is he more famous for?
jokes.setup: Meredith using fake name Van Helsing
jokes.punchline: Dwight defending the academic credentials of a fictional vampire hunter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - taking fictional character's resume seriously

jokes.text: Hey! That's my mug. You know this isn't real TV, right? Yes.
jokes.setup: Kevin seeing his mug in the fake office setup
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to confirm this isn't real TV
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment acknowledging the show's format within the show's universe

jokes.text: Please, Mr. President... (falsetto song about little girl asking president to bring troops home)
jokes.setup: Andy performing his original song
jokes.punchline: Grown man singing in falsetto from child's perspective about war
jokes.explanation: Good cringe content showing Andy's tone-deaf artistic pretensions

jokes.text: People can't keep their true natures hidden for long, and this guy is smoldering like a tire fire.
jokes.setup: Dwight watching Danny take off his coat
jokes.punchline: Dwight's over-the-top description comparing attractiveness to tire fire
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight metaphor - taking attraction seriously and describing it with environmental disaster imagery

jokes.text: Yeah, he is that good-looking. Okay? He's very, very handsome.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to deny Danny's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Jim forced to admit Danny's handsomeness with obvious discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's jealousy and discomfort

jokes.text: Oh, my God! He's making her sell to him.
jokes.setup: Watching Danny work his sales magic on Meredith
jokes.punchline: Jim's realization that Danny has reversed the power dynamic
jokes.explanation: Good observation about sales technique and power reversal

jokes.text: That's pandering. And it makes me think you think I'm stupid... It's kind of weird that a grown man is singing from the point of view of a little girl.
jokes.setup: Oscar critiquing Andy's political song
jokes.punchline: Oscar's blunt, intellectual criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar moment - intellectually dissecting Andy's artistic choices

jokes.text: I don't think they usually skate to such bad songs.
jokes.setup: Andy suggesting Olympic ice skating to his song
jokes.punchline: Oscar's deadpan dismissal of the song's quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar deadpan - brutally honest with complete emotional detachment

jokes.text: I've never been a desk man. Always traveling on the road.
jokes.setup: Danny working his charm on fake executive Meredith
jokes.punchline: Smooth line delivery while getting closer physically
jokes.explanation: Standard smooth-talking salesman bit, functional but predictable

jokes.text: Manuel, cleano el window.
jokes.setup: Need to get someone else into the fake office
jokes.punchline: Meredith's terrible fake Spanish to Clark disguised as janitor
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor about bad fake language, functional but not clever

jokes.text: I run Google. Larry and Sergey brought me...
jokes.setup: Clark getting fake backstory as tech executive
jokes.punchline: Claiming to run Google with name-dropping founders
jokes.explanation: Lazy name-dropping humor without much craft or character specificity

jokes.text: You smell like a Scorpio.
jokes.setup: Meredith trying to be seductive with Danny
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical pickup line connecting smell to astrology
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Meredith line - completely nonsensical but perfectly in character

jokes.text: I'm going in! I'm going in! Michael! No, Michael, stop it!
jokes.setup: Danny getting too close to Meredith in their fake setup
jokes.punchline: Michael's panicked intervention like a military operation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and panic moment but fairly standard physical comedy

jokes.text: Okay, so you set up this fake company, then you hired this homeless woman to impersonate an executive, to spy on me so that you could copy my sales technique.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining their convoluted plan
jokes.punchline: Danny's perfect summary making it sound even more ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that makes the absurd plan sound even more ridiculous

jokes.text: There's no tricks, man. I'm just a good salesman. You wanna copy that? You can't copy that!
jokes.setup: Michael asking about Danny's sales tricks
jokes.punchline: Danny's straightforward truth about natural talent
jokes.explanation: Good truth moment that deflates their entire premise

jokes.text: Do you want your life to be better or to be worse or to stay the same? Get out of my way.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to recruit Danny with life philosophy
jokes.punchline: Danny's complete dismissal of Michael's technique
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's questionable sales technique

jokes.text: He's like a better-looking Andy. Thanks, Kevin.
jokes.setup: Michael asking who Danny reminds them of
jokes.punchline: Kevin's backhanded compliment comparing Danny to Andy
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment - innocent cruelty delivered without awareness

jokes.text: Do you want your life to be better or worse or stay the same? ALL: Stay the same.
jokes.setup: Michael using his life philosophy question on the whole office
jokes.punchline: Everyone unanimously choosing status quo
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing office's resistance to Michael's changes

jokes.text: Couldn't get Out of bed today, Wish the alarm clock Would go away
jokes.setup: Andy asking Kevin about universal feelings
jokes.punchline: Kevin's relatable lyrics about depression/laziness
jokes.explanation: Decent relatable humor about mundane depression

jokes.text: I got a closet full of mes
jokes.setup: Kevin continuing his improvised song about identity
jokes.punchline: Clever wordplay on 'me's vs 'me' plural
jokes.explanation: Surprisingly clever wordplay from Kevin that works both grammatically and philosophically

jokes.text: Oh, you know, funny, your wife and I went on a few dates.
jokes.setup: Danny casually mentioning dating Pam
jokes.punchline: The awkward timing and casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with awkward timing but fairly predictable

jokes.text: She is one of the plain, hearty women of Scranton that make this city great.
jokes.setup: Dwight defending Pam from Danny
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment calling her 'plain' while trying to be supportive
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - trying to be helpful but insulting in the process

jokes.text: This morning, Danny Cordray stole a sale from me. So what do I do? I go out and I steal Danny Cordray. The sale that mattered, I made. Boom.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his strategy in confessional
jokes.punchline: Michael's twisted logic making hiring Danny seem like winning
jokes.explanation: Good Michael talking head showing his ability to reframe failure as success

jokes.text: Crap. I forgot about Packer.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing they already have a traveling salesman
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret about hiring Danny
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic realization - Michael's grand gesture immediately deflated by practical reality

jokes.text: Bullfrog in love song with ribbit sound effects
jokes.setup: Andy's novelty frog song
jokes.punchline: The entire ridiculous concept and execution
jokes.explanation: Absurdist content but runs too long and relies on silly sounds rather than clever writing
